officialReferences.name: NHS service manual design system
officialReferences.borrowed: Consistent accessible service grammar, plain content hierarchy, and WCAG 2.2-oriented design-system posture.
officialReferences.rejected: Public-service page scale and large content bands; the staff rail stays dense and workbench-like.

officialReferences.name: WAI-ARIA APG complementary landmark example
officialReferences.borrowed: The rail is an aside/complementary landmark with an accessible label because it supports the main task canvas.
officialReferences.rejected: Unlabelled secondary regions in a dense shell, because the workspace can include several landmark-like regions.

officialReferences.name: WAI-ARIA APG disclosure pattern
officialReferences.borrowed: The collapse control uses a native button with aria-expanded and aria-controls.
officialReferences.rejected: A custom disclosure role or div-button implementation.

officialReferences.name: Playwright ARIA snapshot testing
officialReferences.borrowed: Rail tests include toMatchAriaSnapshot coverage for expanded and collapsed accessible structure.
officialReferences.rejected: Visual-only approval without structural accessibility proof.

officialReferences.name: Playwright browser context isolation
officialReferences.borrowed: Each rail test creates independent contexts for posture fixtures.

officialReferences.name: Playwright visual comparisons
officialReferences.borrowed: Visual spec uses toHaveScreenshot for expanded, collapsed, and narrow-width baselines.
officialReferences.rejected: Ad hoc screenshots without a repeatable assertion.

officialReferences.name: Playwright trace viewer
officialReferences.borrowed: Tests enable trace capture for failure triage and local replay.

officialReferences.name: Playwright emulation
officialReferences.borrowed: Narrow-width and reduced-motion fixtures use browser emulation rather than CSS-only assumptions.

officialReferences.name: Linear concepts and keyboard model
officialReferences.borrowed: Keyboard-first operations, repeatable action patterns, and low-noise command posture.
officialReferences.rejected: Project-management terminology or issue-centric visual metaphors.

officialReferences.name: Linear board layout
officialReferences.borrowed: Dense list/board controls preserve keyboard parity and avoid mouse-only hidden actions.
officialReferences.rejected: Kanban board layout or large column chrome inside the clinical rail.

officialReferences.name: Next.js App Router layouts and pages
officialReferences.borrowed: Persistent shared layout principle: the assistive rail is wired into the workspace shell family rather than a detached page.
officialReferences.rejected: A standalone route shell for assistive output.

officialReferences.name: Carbon keyboard accessibility
officialReferences.borrowed: Logical tab order, native controls, visible focus, and landmark-driven screen-reader navigation.
officialReferences.rejected: Keyboard shortcuts that are not discoverable through standard focus behavior.

officialReferences.name: Carbon search usage
officialReferences.borrowed: Small constrained controls and component-level information density for work surfaces.
officialReferences.rejected: A search-first rail; the first rail is summary and posture first.

officialReferences.name: Carbon data table usage
officialReferences.borrowed: Toolbar restraint and stable inline affordances for dense professional UI.
officialReferences.rejected: Always-visible action clutter or broad table-style chrome.

officialReferences.name: Carbon filtering pattern
officialReferences.borrowed: Selection methods should fit user intent and reduce cognitive work.
officialReferences.rejected: Multiple filter controls in the first rail shell.

implementationDecisions: The live UI uses no decorative AI imagery.
The rail uses an aside complementary landmark, not an overlay dialog.
State color is limited to small chips, borders, and provenance accents.
The collapsed state keeps a stable 56px host rather than removing the rail.
Below 1024px the rail becomes a same-shell bounded side-stage with max-width 92vw.
